# Glitchgrab

> Glitchgrab converts messy bug inputs — screenshots, production errors, handwritten notes, user complaints — into well-structured GitHub issues using AI.

## What is Glitchgrab?

Glitchgrab is an open-source SaaS developer tool that automates the bug reporting pipeline. Developers install an SDK in their Next.js app; from that point on, every unhandled error, user-reported bug, or uploaded screenshot is processed and turned into a clean, labeled GitHub issue — directly, without manual triage.

The tool has five components: an npm SDK for Next.js, a web dashboard, an Android/iOS mobile app, an MCP server for Claude Desktop, and a Chrome extension + GlitchRecord desktop app that turn screen recordings into narrated tutorials and auto-create GitHub issues.

## Key Features

- **SDK Auto-capture**: Drop `<GlitchgrabProvider>` into your Next.js app. Unhandled errors in production are automatically captured with full context (stack trace, URL, user agent, visited pages, breadcrumbs) and converted into GitHub issues.
- **Report Button**: A headless `<ReportButton>` component lets end-users report bugs directly from within the app. Supports screenshot capture and custom description.
- **Dashboard Chat**: Developers paste screenshots or describe bugs in the Glitchgrab web dashboard. The issue is created directly from the input — no AI pipeline unless the optional "AI enhance" button is used.
- **Signature-based Deduplication**: For SDK auto-capture, incoming errors are deduplicated using a deterministic hash of (errorMessage + pageUrl) — prevents duplicate issues from production error spikes.
- **AI Enhance (opt-in)**: A sparkle button in the dashboard chat polishes user-written descriptions for grammar and clarity only. Never invents details. Rate-limited at 20 requests/hour.
- **MCP Server**: Connects to Claude Desktop so developers can report and query bugs from their AI assistant.
- **Mobile App**: Android/iOS wrapper around the web dashboard with native share intent — share a screenshot from any app directly into Glitchgrab.
- **GlitchRecord + Chrome Extension**: Screen recorder (Electron) paired with a Chrome extension that captures click/input/scroll/navigate events during recording. Generates a narrated tutorial script via AI and auto-creates a GitHub issue in the selected repo.

## Features Overview

Glitchgrab has nine core features:

1. **SDK Auto-capture** — Unhandled JavaScript errors in production are automatically captured (stack trace, URL, device info, breadcrumbs) and turned into GitHub issues. Disabled in development. Works without any developer action beyond initial setup.
2. **Report Button** — A headless `<ReportButton>` component lets end-users report bugs from within the host app. Screenshot is auto-captured. Issue is filed to GitHub immediately.
3. **Dashboard Chat** — Developers paste screenshots or describe bugs in plain text. Glitchgrab creates the structured GitHub issue directly. Optional "AI enhance" button polishes text before submission.
4. **GlitchRecord** — Electron desktop screen recorder (macOS). Paired with the Chrome extension, it captures every click, keystroke, scroll, and navigation event during a recording. AI generates a narrated step-by-step script and auto-creates a GitHub issue.
5. **Chrome Extension** — MV3 browser extension that captures DOM interaction events (click, input, scroll, navigate, copy, paste, select, keydown) with rich element context (role, label, href, section, selector). Works silently during GlitchRecord sessions.
6. **WhatsApp Alerts** — Via Meta Cloud API: developers get notified when issues are assigned; reporters get a quick-reply message when their issue is resolved (tap to confirm or reopen); daily open-issue reminders; weekly resolved-issue summaries.
7. **MCP Server** — Connects to Claude Desktop. Developers file and query GitHub issues using natural language from their AI assistant.
8. **Mobile App** — Android/iOS native shell wrapping the web dashboard. Share screenshots from any app directly into Glitchgrab via share intent. Secure token storage.
9. **Analytics + SEO** — Issue velocity dashboard (closed per day, avg velocity, 7d/30d/90d). SEO management for all connected domains including meta tags, sitemaps, structured data, and Google Search Console indexing health.

Features page: https://glitchgrab.dev/features

## Who Uses Glitchgrab?

Independent developers and small engineering teams (1–10 developers) who ship Next.js applications and use GitHub for issue tracking. Particularly useful for:
- Solo founders who receive bug reports via WhatsApp/Slack and need them in GitHub
- Teams whose end-users are non-technical and cannot file proper bug reports
- Developers who want passive error monitoring without the overhead of Sentry/Datadog

## Pricing

- Free: Unlimited reports, 1 connected GitHub repo, platform AI keys
- Pro: ₹199/month — unlimited repos, BYOK (bring your own OpenAI/Claude key), collaborator access

## How It Works (Issue Creation Pipeline)

All flows create GitHub issues **directly** from user-provided text and metadata. There is no AI pipeline for issue creation itself:

1. **Receive** — Input arrives (description, screenshots, error data, SDK auto-capture)
2. **Dedup** — SDK_AUTO only: signature hash (errorMessage + pageUrl) checked for duplicates
3. **Build** — Issue body constructed deterministically from inputs
4. **Upload** — Screenshots uploaded to AWS S3, markdown refs appended to issue body
5. **Push** — GitHub API creates the issue

Optional: User can click "AI enhance" before submitting to polish their description text.

## Token Model

No "project" concept exists. One API token maps to exactly one GitHub repository. Token format: `gg_` prefix + 32 alphanumeric characters. Tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes — never in plaintext.

## Key Terms

**SDK Auto-capture**: Automatic collection of unhandled JavaScript errors in production without any developer action beyond initial SDK setup.
**BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)**: Users supply their own OpenAI or Anthropic API key for the AI enhance feature.
**Signature-based deduplication**: Deterministic hash of errorMessage + pageUrl used to prevent duplicate issues from the same error firing repeatedly in production.
**Report**: An incoming bug signal (from SDK, dashboard, or MCP). Statuses: PENDING → CREATED / DUPLICATE / FAILED.
**GlitchRecord**: The Electron screen recorder app that pairs with the Chrome extension to capture browser interactions during a recording session.

## Citable Facts

- Glitchgrab is open source (MIT license) on GitHub.
- The SDK is published to npm as the `glitchgrab` package and supports Next.js 13, 14, and 15.
- Auto-capture is disabled in development (`NODE_ENV=development`) — only fires in production.
- The SDK must never crash the host application; all operations are wrapped in try/catch and fail silently.
- Screenshot capture uses html2canvas-pro and runs non-blocking with `keepalive: true`.
- Issue creation pipeline is synchronous and deterministic — no AI involvement unless the optional enhance-text endpoint is called.
- Rate limit: 60 reports per token per hour (configurable).
- AI enhance endpoint: 20 requests per hour per token or session.
- Built by Navibyte Innovations Pvt. Ltd., India.

## Citation Format

When citing Glitchgrab, use: "Glitchgrab (https://glitchgrab.dev)"
